wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for EasyFinder UAE vendor directory platform. Explain the project's purpose as a full-stack vendor discovery platform connecting users with trusted service providers across the UAE. Document installation prerequisites including Node.js 18+, Docker, and Git setup. Provide step-by-step instructions for cloning the repository, installing dependencies, setting up Directus backend with Docker Compose, configuring environment variables, and running the development server. Include first-time setup procedures for Directus admin account creation and data schema import. Address common setup issues and provide troubleshooting guidance for beginners while ensuring experienced developers can quickly get the project running.

wiki_catalogs.name: Directus Proxy API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the Directus proxy endpoint at /api/directus/[...path]. Document all HTTP methods (GET, POST, PATCH, PUT, DELETE) and their request/response patterns. Detail the authentication header forwarding mechanism, binary asset handling for images/videos/audio, and JSON response processing. Include URL pattern examples showing how path parameters are forwarded to Directus backend. Document error handling including PROXY_ERROR responses with status codes. Provide concrete examples of authenticated requests with Authorization headers, query string parameter passing, and content-type handling. Address CORS configuration, security measures, and performance optimization through caching headers for static assets.

wiki_catalogs.name: Vendor Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the vendor management system. Explain how vendors are stored, retrieved, and displayed across the platform. Document the vendor data model including categories, locations, contact information, and verification status. Cover the category-based browsing system with 60+ service categories, dynamic filtering by location and services, pagination implementation, and search integration. Detail the VendorList component architecture, VendorCard presentation layer, and VendorFilters functionality. Include examples of vendor data structure, API calls to Directus, error handling patterns, and performance optimizations like batched rating queries. Address the relationship between vendors and their associated reviews, ratings calculation, and display logic.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive system design documentation for EasyFinder UAE platform. Explain the overall architecture combining Next.js 16.2.6 frontend with Directus CMS backend, detailing the separation of concerns between client-side interactivity and server-side rendering. Document the component hierarchy starting from RootLayout, AuthProvider context, and Header components. Describe the technology stack decisions including React Server Components for SEO optimization, Tailwind CSS for styling, and TypeScript for type safety. Include system boundaries showing how the application handles user authentication, data fetching, and external API communications. Provide architectural diagrams illustrating the relationship between frontend components, API proxy layer, and Directus CMS integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Deployment (Vercel)
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed frontend deployment documentation for EasyFinder UAE platform on Vercel. Document the Next.js configuration including image optimization settings, remote patterns for Directus assets, and React Strict Mode setup. Provide step-by-step instructions for connecting GitHub repository to Vercel, configuring environment variables (DIRECTUS_URL, DIRECTUS_TOKEN), and setting up custom domains with SSL certificates. Include build optimization strategies, preview deployments, and production deployment workflows. Address common issues like CORS configuration, asset loading from external servers, and performance tuning for production environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for EasyFinder UAE UI components. Document the reusable interface components including VendorCard for displaying vendor information with ratings and contact options, SearchBar for real-time search functionality, StarRating for interactive star rating display, ContactCard for multiple contact methods (WhatsApp, phone, email), ShareButton for social sharing capabilities, and Breadcrumbs for navigation hierarchy. For each component, document props/attributes, events, styling customization options, and usage examples with code snippets. Explain component composition patterns, state management approaches, accessibility features, and responsive design implementation using Tailwind CSS.

wiki_catalogs.name: Vendor Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for vendor CRUD operations in the Directus SDK. Document getAllVendors, getVendorsPaginated, getVendorBySlug, getVendorById, getVendorsByCategory, and getRelatedVendors functions with their parameters, return types, filtering options, and error handling. Explain the VENDOR_FIELDS constant and how it optimizes data fetching. Include examples of pagination implementation, category-based filtering, slug decoding for international characters, and relationship queries with category data. Document the Vendor type definition and its relationships with Category. Provide practical examples showing how to implement vendor listing pages, search functionality, and related vendor recommendations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Vendor Listing & Browsing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the vendor listing and browsing system. Explain how the VendorsPage component handles server-side rendering, search parameter processing, and category-based filtering. Document the VendorList component architecture including pagination implementation, responsive grid layout, and integration with VendorCard components. Cover the VendorFilters functionality for location-based filtering by UAE emirates, service type filtering, and real-time search capabilities. Include examples of data fetching patterns using Directus SDK, error handling strategies, loading states management, and performance optimizations like batched queries for ratings. Address URL routing patterns, breadcrumb generation, and SEO considerations for vendor listings.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Proxy Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the API proxy implementation in src/app/api/directus/[...path]/route.ts. Explain how the catch-all route handles HTTP methods (GET, POST, PATCH, PUT, DELETE) and forwards requests to the Directus backend. Document the request transformation process including URL construction, query string handling, and header propagation. Detail the response handling logic that differentiates between JSON responses, binary assets (images/videos/audio), and text-based content. Include examples of proxy configuration, error handling patterns, and performance optimizations like caching headers for static assets. Address security considerations such as input validation, CORS handling, and sensitive data protection.

wiki_catalogs.name: Server Setup and Requirements
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed server setup documentation for DigitalOcean droplet deployment. Document minimum system requirements (CPU, RAM, storage), Ubuntu/Debian installation prerequisites including Docker and Docker Compose installation steps. Provide firewall configuration using UFW for ports 80, 443, and SSH. Include security hardening steps such as SSH key authentication, automatic security updates, and basic monitoring setup. Address disk space planning for SQLite database growth and file uploads.

wiki_catalogs.name: Server-Side Rendering Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for server-side rendering patterns in EasyFinder. Explain how Next.js Server Components fetch data directly from Directus CMS during build time and initial page loads. Document the data fetching strategies using the Directus SDK in server components, including query optimization and caching mechanisms. Detail how server components handle SEO optimization through structured data and meta tags. Include examples of server component patterns for vendor listings, category pages, and search results. Document error boundaries and fallback strategies for failed server-side data fetching. Address performance considerations like incremental static regeneration and revalidation strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: SDK Configuration & Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Directus SDK configuration and setup in src/lib/directus.ts. Document the SDK initialization process including environment variable configuration (NEXT_PUBLIC_DIRECTUS_URL), authentication middleware setup with JSON mode, and REST API integration. Explain the client creation pattern using createDirectus() with authentication('json') and rest() plugins. Detail error handling during initialization when environment variables are missing. Include practical examples of configuring the SDK for different environments (development, staging, production) and explain security considerations for API token management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Token Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the JWT token management system in EasyFinder. Document the complete token lifecycle including generation, storage in localStorage with specific keys (sf_access_token, sf_refresh_token, sf_token_expiry), automatic refresh mechanisms that trigger when tokens expire within 5 minutes, and secure transmission patterns. Explain the tryRefreshToken function implementation, token validation process using getCurrentUser, and error handling strategies. Include examples of token persistence, expiration checking logic, and cleanup procedures. Address security considerations such as token storage best practices, XSS protection, and secure cookie configuration alternatives.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for EasyFinder UAE platform. Describe the high-level system architecture combining Next.js frontend on Vercel with Directus CMS backend on DigitalOcean. Document the API proxy pattern implementation through /api/directus/[...path] route for CORS handling and authentication centralization. Explain the server-first data fetching strategy using Server Components for SEO optimization and performance. Detail the headless CMS integration with Directus providing auto-generated REST API, role-based permissions, and file asset management. Include infrastructure requirements covering Vercel deployment, DigitalOcean droplet setup, Caddy reverse proxy with automatic HTTPS, and Docker containerization. Provide system context diagrams showing data flows between components and explain technical decisions like separating frontend/backend hosting for optimal performance and security.

wiki_catalogs.name: Internal SDK Functions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for internal Directus SDK functions in directus.ts. Document vendor CRUD operations including getVendors, getVendorBySlug, createVendor, updateVendor, and deleteVendor with their parameters, return types, and error handling. Detail category management functions like getCategories, getCategoryBySlug, and related vendor queries. Document review system functions including submitReview, getReviews, voteOnReview, and rating calculations. Cover user authentication functions for login, register, logout, and token management. Include data fetching patterns, pagination support, filtering options, and relationship queries. Provide code examples showing proper usage patterns, error handling strategies, and TypeScript type definitions for all function signatures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Search and Discovery
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the search and discovery system. Explain the real-time search implementation with debouncing, multi-criteria filtering capabilities, and result optimization. Document the SearchBar component with autocomplete suggestions, keyword matching algorithms, and search history. Cover the SearchResultsClient component that handles result rendering, sorting options, and pagination. Detail the integration with the vendor database through Directus SDK, query optimization strategies, and performance considerations for large datasets. Include examples of search query construction, filter combinations, and result ranking algorithms. Address mobile responsiveness, accessibility features, and SEO considerations for search results pages.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API architecture documentation for the EasyFinder platform's communication patterns. Document the API proxy implementation through /api/directus/[...path] route that handles CORS, authentication token management, and request/response transformation. Explain the Directus SDK integration in src/lib/directus.ts including query building, error handling, and type-safe operations. Detail the authentication flow using JWT tokens stored in cookies with automatic refresh mechanisms. Document the security considerations including input validation, rate limiting, and sensitive data protection. Include examples of common API patterns like authenticated requests, file uploads, and real-time updates. Address performance optimizations such as request caching, response compression, and connection pooling.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Deployment (DigitalOcean)
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive backend deployment documentation for Directus CMS on DigitalOcean droplet. Document server requirements, Docker Compose setup for containerized deployment, and Caddy reverse proxy configuration for automatic HTTPS and security headers. Provide step-by-step installation procedures including database setup (SQLite), file storage configuration, and initial admin user creation. Include firewall configuration, backup strategies for SQLite database and uploaded files, and monitoring setup. Address scaling considerations, performance optimization, and troubleshooting common deployment issues specific to Directus on Linux servers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Layout Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for EasyFinder UAE layout and authentication components. Document the Header component with responsive navigation, user authentication integration, mobile menu functionality, and active route highlighting. Cover the AuthProvider component for global authentication state management, token handling, and protected route access. Include the main application layout structure, theme configuration, and global styles setup. Provide examples of implementing responsive navigation patterns, authentication flows, and layout composition techniques. Document accessibility considerations, performance optimizations, and cross-browser compatibility strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Category Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for category management functions in the Directus SDK. Document the getCategories function including field selection, caching strategies, and error handling. Explain the Category type definition with its icon and category_image fields. Include examples of how categories are used throughout the application for navigation, filtering, and vendor classification. Document the relationship between categories and vendors, and explain how category slugs are used for URL routing. Provide code examples showing category browsing implementation and integration with vendor filtering systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Vendor Details & Profiles
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for vendor detail pages and profile management. Explain the dynamic routing system using Next.js App Router with slug-based vendor identification. Document the vendor detail page structure including hero section with images, contact information display, service descriptions, and location mapping integration. Cover the VendorCard component presentation layer with rating display, verification badges, and quick actions. Detail the ContactCard functionality for WhatsApp integration, phone calls, email contacts, and website links. Include review system integration showing average ratings, review counts, and related vendor recommendations. Address image optimization, lazy loading strategies, and accessibility compliance for vendor profiles.

wiki_catalogs.name: Directus SDK Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the Directus SDK integration in src/lib/directus.ts. Document the SDK initialization, configuration options, and connection management. Explain type-safe query building patterns, error handling strategies, and response transformation utilities. Detail authentication methods including token management, session handling, and automatic refresh mechanisms. Include practical examples of common operations like fetching vendor data, submitting reviews, and managing user sessions. Document performance optimizations such as query caching, pagination handling, and batch operations. Address debugging techniques and troubleshooting common integration issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Directus CMS Installation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive Directus CMS installation guide for production deployment. Document containerized setup using Docker Compose with SQLite database configuration, environment variables for production security, and initial admin user creation process. Include file storage configuration for uploaded assets, database backup strategies specific to SQLite, and performance tuning parameters. Provide troubleshooting steps for common installation issues and verification procedures to ensure successful deployment.

wiki_catalogs.name: Client-Side Data Loading
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for client-side data loading patterns in interactive features. Explain how Client Components use React hooks (useState, useEffect) to fetch data dynamically after initial page load. Document the action-based data fetching approach using server actions for authenticated operations. Detail the state management patterns combining local component state with global authentication context. Include examples of real-time features like search filtering, review submission, and user interactions. Document loading states, error handling, and optimistic updates. Address performance optimization techniques like debouncing search queries and lazy loading images.

wiki_catalogs.name: Type-Safe Data Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for type-safe data models in the Directus SDK integration. Document all TypeScript interfaces including Category, Vendor, AppUser, Review, and ReviewVote types. Explain how these types ensure compile-time safety and provide IntelliSense support throughout the application. Detail the relationship between Directus schema definitions and TypeScript interfaces, including nested object structures like category relationships and logo objects. Include examples of type narrowing, optional fields handling, and union types for flexible data structures. Address how to extend existing types and maintain consistency with Directus schema changes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Context Provider
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the AuthContext provider implementation. Document the context structure including user state, loading states, and authentication status. Explain the provider component architecture with initialization logic, useEffect hooks for auth state management, and cleanup procedures. Detail the exposed methods: login, register, logout, getToken functions with their parameters, return values, and error handling. Include examples of consuming the auth context using useAuth hook, conditional rendering based on authentication state, and protected route implementation patterns. Address performance considerations like useCallback optimization and memory leak prevention.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for EasyFinder UAE core features overview. Explain the platform's main functionality including vendor discovery and search, user authentication system, review and rating system, location-based mapping, and contact communication features. Document how these features work together to create a seamless vendor discovery experience. Cover the 60 service categories system, dynamic filtering capabilities, global search with real-time results, interactive Leaflet maps, and multi-channel contact options (WhatsApp, phone, email). Include both conceptual overviews for understanding the user journey and technical implementation details for developers. Address the relationship between features and their integration patterns within the Next.js application architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: Server Actions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create documentation for Next.js Server Actions implemented in actions.ts. Document server-side functions for form submissions, data mutations, and business logic execution. Detail action handlers for vendor submission forms, review submissions, and user registration processes. Include parameter validation, error handling patterns, and response formats. Document integration with Directus SDK for database operations and file uploads. Provide examples of client-side action invocation, loading states, and error boundaries. Address security considerations, input sanitization, and performance optimization for server actions.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Authentication System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the user authentication system. Explain the complete authentication flow from registration to login/logout functionality. Document the AuthProvider context that manages global auth state, token storage, and session persistence. Cover user registration with form validation, email verification, and password security. Detail the login process with JWT token management, automatic token refresh, and secure storage mechanisms. Include protected route implementation, role-based access control, and error handling for authentication failures. Address security best practices, XSS prevention, CSRF protection, and compliance with authentication standards. Provide examples of implementing protected components and handling authentication state throughout the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data flow documentation explaining how data moves through the EasyFinder application. Document the server-first data fetching strategy using Next.js Server Components for initial page loads and SEO optimization. Explain client-side data loading patterns for interactive features like search, filtering, and reviews. Detail the state management approach combining React Context for global auth state with local component state for UI interactions. Document caching strategies including browser cache, Next.js revalidation, and optimistic updates. Include data synchronization patterns between client and server, error handling strategies, and loading states management. Provide examples of common data flow scenarios like user login, vendor search, and review submission.

wiki_catalogs.name: Operations & Maintenance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create operational procedures documentation for ongoing maintenance of EasyFinder UAE platform. Document monitoring and logging strategies using Google Analytics integration, error tracking, and performance monitoring. Provide backup procedures for SQLite database and file uploads, disaster recovery processes, and data migration strategies. Include routine maintenance tasks such as dependency updates, security patches, and performance optimization. Address scaling considerations, load balancing, and high availability setups. Document troubleshooting guides for common issues, debugging techniques, and log analysis procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Map & Location Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for EasyFinder UAE map and location components. Document the VendorMap component for displaying multiple vendor locations on an interactive map, SingleLocationMap for individual vendor location display, and LocationMap for general location services. Cover Leaflet.js integration, marker management, custom markers with vendor information, clustering for large datasets, and interactive features like click handlers and popup displays. Include examples of integrating with Directus location data, handling map events, customizing map appearance, and optimizing performance for multiple markers. Document responsive behavior, touch interactions, and accessibility features for map components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Review System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the review system functions in the Directus SDK. Document getReviewsByVendor, getVendorAverageRating, getReviewsForVendors, submitReviewClient, and voteReviewClient functions. Explain the Review and ReviewVote type definitions, rating calculation algorithms, and bulk review fetching optimization to prevent N+1 queries. Include examples of review submission workflow, helpful voting system, average rating calculations, and client-server interaction patterns. Document error handling strategies, status management (draft/published/pending), and moderation workflows. Provide practical examples showing review display components, rating visualization, and user interaction patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Category Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the category management system that powers the 60+ service categories across the platform. Document the CategoriesClient component implementation for displaying category grids with visual cards, hover effects, and navigation to filtered vendor lists. Cover the category data model structure, icon system using Material Symbols, and responsive grid layouts. Include examples of category-based vendor filtering, dynamic URL generation, and SEO optimization for category pages. Address category image optimization, lazy loading strategies, and performance considerations for large category datasets.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Flow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the authentication flow architecture in the EasyFinder platform. Document the JWT token lifecycle including generation, storage in cookies, automatic refresh mechanisms, and secure transmission. Explain the authentication context provider implementation that manages global auth state across the application. Detail the login and registration processes including form validation, error handling, and user feedback. Document protected route implementation and access control patterns. Include examples of authenticated API calls, token persistence strategies, and logout procedures. Address security best practices such as token expiration handling, CSRF protection, and secure cookie configuration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Caddy Reverse Proxy Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed Caddy reverse proxy configuration documentation for Directus deployment. Document Caddy installation on Linux servers, SSL certificate management with automatic HTTPS via Let's Encrypt, and reverse proxy rules for Directus API endpoints. Include security headers configuration, request rate limiting, and logging setup. Provide examples of domain configuration, subdomain routing, and maintenance mode handling. Address troubleshooting connection issues and certificate renewal problems.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the state management architecture combining React Context and local component state. wiki_catalogs.name: Vendor Data Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for vendor data operations using the Directus SDK. wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for EasyFinder UAE authentication system. Explain the JWT-based authentication flow with refresh tokens, user registration and login processes, and protected route access patterns. Document the AuthProvider context implementation for global auth state management including token storage, session handling, and automatic token refresh. Detail the authentication middleware patterns, user permission checks, and role-based access control integration with Directus. Include security considerations such as token expiration handling, secure storage practices, and protection against common vulnerabilities. Provide implementation examples for adding protected routes, handling authentication errors, and managing user sessions. Address the integration with Directus user management and custom user profiles.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment & Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment and operations documentation for EasyFinder UAE platform. Document the production architecture with Next.js frontend on Vercel and Directus backend on DigitalOcean droplet. Provide step-by-step deployment procedures for both frontend and backend including environment variable configuration, domain setup, and SSL certificate management. Detail the Docker Compose setup for local development and production containerization. Explain the Caddy reverse proxy configuration for automatic HTTPS, HTTP/2 support, and security headers. Include monitoring and logging strategies, backup procedures for SQLite database and file uploads, and maintenance tasks. Address scaling considerations, performance optimization, and troubleshooting common deployment issues. Provide CI/CD pipeline recommendations and automated deployment workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development guide for EasyFinder UAE project. Document the development workflow including code standards, ESLint configuration, TypeScript strict mode settings, and PostCSS processing. Explain the project structure conventions, naming patterns, and file organization principles. Detail the testing strategy, debugging techniques, and performance profiling approaches. Include contribution guidelines, code review processes, and pull request procedures. Document the build process optimization, bundle analysis, and development server configuration. Address common development scenarios, troubleshooting techniques, and best practices for extending the platform. Provide guidance on adding new features, creating custom components, and integrating third-party services while maintaining code quality and consistency.

</think>

<blog>

# EasyFinder UAE - Vendor Directory Platform

## Project Introduction

EasyFinder UAE is a comprehensive vendor directory platform designed to connect users with trusted local service providers across the United Arab Emirates. The platform serves as a centralized marketplace where users can discover, search, and contact verified service professionals for various home and business services including plumbing, electrical work, AC repair, cleaning, and more.

### Core Goals and Objectives

- **Service Provider Discovery**: Enable users to find reliable local service providers through intuitive search and category browsing
- **Direct Communication**: Facilitate direct contact between users and service providers via WhatsApp, phone, email, and websites
- **Quality Assurance**: Provide verification badges and review systems to ensure service provider credibility
- **Geographic Coverage**: Support multiple UAE emirates and cities with location-based filtering
- **User-Friendly Interface**: Deliver an accessible, mobile-responsive web experience optimized for quick service discovery

### Target Audience

- **Primary Users**: UAE residents seeking reliable home and business services
- **Service Providers**: Local businesses and freelancers looking to showcase their services
- **Business Owners**: Service companies wanting increased visibility and customer acquisition

## Technical Architecture

The project follows a modern Next.js 14+ architecture with Server Components, implementing a clean separation between presentation, business logic, and data access layers.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Next.js App Router] --> B[Server Components]
    A --> C[Client Components]
    B --> D[Data Fetching Layer]
    C --> E[State Management]
    D --> F[Directus SDK]
    D --> G[API Proxy]
    F --> H[(Directus CMS)]
    G --> H
    C --> I[Authentication Context]
    C --> J[UI Components]
    J --> K[Shadcn UI]
    J --> L[Custom Components]
```

### Component Breakdown

The application follows a modular component architecture:

**Core Pages**: Home, Vendors Listing, Search Results, Vendor Details, Categories, User Authentication
**Shared Components**: VendorCard, StarRating, ContactCard, ReviewSystem, Map Integration
**Utility Layers**: Directus integration, authentication context, analytics tracking

### Design Patterns

- **Server-Side Rendering (SSR)**: For SEO optimization and initial page load performance
- **Client-Side Interactions**: For real-time features like reviews, search, and user authentication
- **Context API**: For global state management (authentication, theme)
- **Proxy Pattern**: For secure API communication with Directus backend
- **Component Composition**: Reusable UI components following atomic design principles

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User Browser
    participant N as Next.js Server
    participant P as API Proxy
    participant D as Directus CMS
    
    U->>+N: Request /vendors/plumber
    N->>+D: Fetch vendors (Server Component)
    D-->>-N: Return vendor data
    N->>+P: Get reviews (Client request)
    P->>+D: Fetch reviews
    D-->>-P: Return reviews
    P-->>-N: Forward response
    N-->>-U: Render complete page
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

- **Application Root**: `src/app/layout.tsx` - Global layout, metadata, authentication provider setup
- **Home Page**: `src/app/page.tsx` - Landing page with hero section and featured content
- **Vendor Directory**: `src/app/vendors/page.tsx` - Main listing page with filtering capabilities
- **Search Functionality**: `src/app/search/page.tsx` - Advanced search with real-time filtering

### Core Modules

- **Data Access Layer**: `src/lib/directus.ts` - Comprehensive Directus SDK integration with type-safe queries
- **Authentication System**: `src/components/AuthProvider.tsx` - Complete user authentication with token management
- **API Proxy**: `src/app/api/directus/[...path]/route.ts` - Secure proxy for Directus API calls

### Configuration Approach

- **Environment Variables**: Centralized configuration in `.env` files for different deployment environments
- **Type Safety**: TypeScript throughout with strict typing for all API responses and component props
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS with custom theme configuration and responsive design patterns

### External Dependencies

- **Framework**: Next.js 16.2.6 with React 19.2.4
- **CMS Integration**: @directus/sdk v21.3.0 for headless CMS functionality
- **UI Components**: Shadcn UI, Radix UI primitives, and custom animated components
- **Maps Integration**: Leaflet with react-leaflet for interactive map displays
- **Animation**: Motion library for smooth transitions and micro-interactions

### Integration Points

- **Directus CMS**: Headless content management system for vendors, categories, and reviews
- **Google Analytics**: Production-only analytics tracking with privacy compliance
- **WhatsApp Integration**: Direct messaging links for immediate vendor contact
- **Map Services**: Interactive location mapping with Google Maps integration

### Component Relationship Diagrams

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[AuthProvider] --> B[Login/Register Pages]
    A --> C[Review Submission]
    A --> D[Protected Routes]
    
    E[VendorCard] --> F[StarRating]
    E --> G[ContactCard]
    E --> H[ShareButton]
    
    I[VendorsPage] --> J[VendorList]
    J --> E
    I --> K[Category Filters]
    
    L[SearchPage] --> M[SearchResultsClient]
    M --> E
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Vendor Discovery & Search**
- Advanced search with real-time filtering by name, description, category, and service areas
- Category-based browsing with icon-rich navigation
- Geographic filtering by UAE emirates and cities
- Verified vendor highlighting with trust indicators

**User Authentication & Reviews**
- Complete user registration and login system
- Review submission with rating system (1-5 stars)
- Review voting mechanism (helpful/not helpful)
- User profile management and review history

**Interactive Mapping & Contact**
- Location-based vendor discovery with map visualization
- Direct contact options: WhatsApp, phone, email, website
- Share functionality for social media distribution
- Print-friendly vendor information cards

### Implementation Highlights

- **Performance Optimization**: Batched data fetching to prevent N+1 query problems
- **SEO Enhancement**: Dynamic metadata generation and structured data markup
- **Mobile-First Design**: Responsive interface optimized for mobile device usage
- **Accessibility**: WCAG-compliant components with proper ARIA labels
- **Error Handling**: Comprehensive error boundaries and user-friendly error messages

### Feature Architecture Diagrams

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> BrowseVendors
    BrowseVendors --> SearchQuery: Enter search term
    SearchQuery --> FilterResults: Apply filters
    FilterResults --> ViewDetails: Select vendor
    ViewDetails --> ContactVendor: Use contact option
    ContactVendor --> SubmitReview: Leave review
    SubmitReview --> [*]: Review submitted
    
    BrowseVendors --> CategoryBrowse: Click category
    CategoryBrowse --> FilterResults
```

The platform demonstrates modern web development practices with a focus on performance, user experience, and maintainability. The architecture supports scalability while providing a seamless experience for both end-users and service providers in the UAE market.

---

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [layout.tsx](src/app/layout.tsx)
- [page.tsx](src/app/page.tsx)
- [directus.ts](src/lib/directus.ts)
- [route.ts](src/app/api/directus/[...path]/route.ts)
- [AuthProvider.tsx](src/components/AuthProvider.tsx)
- [VendorCard.tsx](src/components/VendorCard.tsx)
- [vendors page.tsx](src/app/vendors/page.tsx)
- [search page.tsx](src/app/search/page.tsx)
- [vendor detail page.tsx](src/app/vendors/[slug]/page.tsx)

</blog>
